sections.paragraph:
ἐξουσιαστικός, ή, όν (Eccl 8, 4 Sym; Vett. Val. 6, 3, Iamblichus.-ώτερον Polyb. 5, 26, 3) pert. to being free from external approval, authoritative Mk 1:27 v.l. (cp. doctrina inpotentabilis It. cod. e).

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

It. It. = Itala, early version of the NT in Latin

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 354
